metadata.title: Pre-GCP Fixes: Checkpoint Rearchitecture (#878) + TopoLoRA Paper Fidelity (#879)
metadata.priority: P0 — last fixes before GCP factorial re-run

context.summary:
Two issues block the GCP factorial re-run:
1. #878 (P0): Checkpoints lost on ephemeral spot VMs — need GCS persistence + atomic writes + SHA256 verification + spot resume wiring
2. #879 (REFRAMED): TopoLoRA-SAM implementation deviates from Khazem et al. (2025). The REAL gap is a missing Spatial Adapter, NOT LoRAConv2d. Issue #879 should be relabeled. Loss must be config-driven for factorial.
After this plan: checkpoints persist to GCS, TopoLoRA matches the paper, and we can run the full 24-condition factorial on GCP.

paper-reference.title: TopoLoRA-SAM: Topology-Aware Low-Rank Adaptation for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
paper-reference.key-architecture: 1. Frozen SAM ViT encoder (paper: ViT-B; ours: ViT-32L SAM3) 2. Trainable LoRA on FFN layers ONLY (mlp.lin1, mlp.lin2) — NOT Conv2d 3. Trainable Spatial Adapter: Conv_DW(3x3) + Conv(1x1) + residual (~66K params) 4. Trainable Mask Decoder 5. Loss: BCE + Dice + 0.5*clDice (topology-aware, config-driven)
paper-reference.our-adaptation: We adapt the technique to SAM3 ViT-32L (648M params) instead of SAM1 ViT-B (93.7M). The LoRA + Spatial Adapter + topology-aware loss architecture is identical. The loss function is CONFIGURABLE — all 3 factorial losses can be used. This is a PLATFORM paper (Nature Protocols), not a SOTA paper. The contribution is showing that the MLOps platform handles any model/loss/adapter combination.

context.local-debug-results: Local 3-flow debug PASSED: 4/4 conditions, 7 bugs found and fixed. DynUNet inference: 7.6s/vol, total 12.5 min for 96 inferences. Key finding: DSC vs clDice rank inversion (cbdice_cldice wins clDice, dice_ce wins DSC). This IS a paper finding.

phase.name: H1: Direct GCS Artifact Upload (15 min)
phase.description:
Switch train_factorial.yaml from file-based /app/mlruns to the Cloud Run MLflow URI with --no-serve-artifacts. Clients upload directly to GCS, bypassing the 32MB Cloud Run limit.
ALREADY DONE: - Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp has --no-serve-artifacts ✓ - smoke_test_gcp.yaml uses ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} ✓ - google-cloud-storage in Docker image ✓ - GCP VMs get ADC automatically via SkyPilot ✓
REMAINING: - train_factorial.yaml: switch MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI from /app/mlruns to ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (the Cloud Run URL)

tasks.task: Change from: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: /app/mlruns To: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} This makes it read from the .env file (Cloud Run URL for GCP, file-based for local).

task.title: Test: verify train_factorial.yaml uses env var

tasks.task: Add test in test_train_factorial_yaml.py: assert "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI" value is "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" assert it does NOT hardcode "/app/mlruns"

phase.name: H6: GCS Bucket Mount for Checkpoints (1 hour)
phase.description: Mount gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints as a file_mount in SkyPilot YAML. Checkpoints written to /checkpoints/ are automatically synced to GCS. Already done in smoke_test_gcp.yaml — port to train_factorial.yaml.

tasks.task: In train_factorial.yaml: file_mounts: /checkpoints: source: gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints mode: MOUNT
Ensure checkpoint_dir reads from CHECKPOINT_DIR env var (default /checkpoints/ in Docker, outputs/checkpoints locally).
Test that train_flow reads CHECKPOINT_DIR from env, not hardcoded.

phase.name: Atomic Checkpoint Writes (#714, 1.5 hours)
phase.description: Prevent corrupted checkpoints from interrupted writes (spot preemption, OOM, SIGTERM). Use sync→rename atomic pattern.

task.title: RED: Test checkpoint write is atomic

tasks.task: Test that save_checkpoint writes to .tmp then renames. Verify: incomplete write never produces a valid checkpoint filename.

task.title: GREEN: Implement atomic save in trainer

tasks.task: In trainer checkpoint save: tmp_path = checkpoint_path.with_suffix(".tmp") torch.save(state_dict, tmp_path) os.fsync(tmp_path.open().fileno()) tmp_path.rename(checkpoint_path)

phase.gate: Checkpoints use atomic write pattern. No partial files.

phase.name: SHA256 Checkpoint Verification (#707, 1 hour)
phase.description: Compute SHA256 hash after save, store as sidecar .sha256 file. Verify on load. Catches silent corruption from GCS sync issues.

tasks.task: After saving, .sha256 file must exist alongside checkpoint. On load, verify hash matches.
save: compute hashlib.sha256, write to {ckpt}.sha256 load: read sidecar, compute hash of loaded file, compare Mismatch: raise CheckpointCorruptionError (not silent)

phase.gate: Every checkpoint has SHA256 sidecar. Load verifies integrity.

phase.name: Spot Preemption Resumption (2 hours)
phase.description: Wire the existing check_resume_state_task() to actually work. On spot preemption recovery, SkyPilot relaunches the job. The flow must detect the last checkpoint and resume from that epoch.

task.title: RED: Test resume detects last checkpoint

tasks.task: Given a checkpoint dir with epoch_5.pth and epoch_10.pth, check_resume_state() returns epoch=10 and the path.
Scan checkpoint_dir for epoch_*.pth files. Return latest epoch number and path. If no checkpoints: return epoch=0 (start fresh).
At flow start: call check_resume_state() If resume: load checkpoint, set start_epoch If fresh: start from epoch 0
Save a checkpoint at epoch 5, call train_flow with resume=True, verify it starts from epoch 5.

phase.gate: Train flow resumes from last checkpoint after spot preemption.

phase.name: Relabel #879 + Document Paper Architecture (30 min)
phase.description: Issue #879 says "Implement LoRAConv2d" but the paper does NOT LoRA Conv2d. The real gap is the missing Spatial Adapter. Relabel the issue, update CLAUDE.md, KG, and everywhere with the correct architecture.

tasks.task: Change title from "P2: Implement LoRAConv2d for Conv2d layers" to "Implement Spatial Adapter + verify TopoLoRA paper fidelity" Update body with the paper reference and diagram.

Add to CLAUDE.md or adapters CLAUDE.md: - TopoLoRA-SAM reference (Khazem et al., 2025) - Architecture: frozen encoder + LoRA FFN + Spatial Adapter + decoder - LoRA targets: FFN (mlp.lin1/lin2) ONLY, not Conv2d or attention - Spatial Adapter: Conv_DW(3x3) + Conv(1x1) + residual - Our adaptation: SAM3 ViT-32L instead of SAM1 ViT-B
Add to knowledge-graph/bibliography.yaml and decisions/L3-technology/paper_model_comparison.yaml.

phase.name: Implement Spatial Adapter (2 hours)
phase.description:
Add the Spatial Adapter (Conv_DW 3x3 + Conv 1x1 + BN + GELU + residual) between the SAM3 encoder output and the mask decoder input.
From the paper/SegLab code: class ConvAdapter(nn.Module): dw = Conv2d(ch, ch, 3, groups=ch) # depthwise pw1 = Conv2d(ch, hidden, 1) # pointwise pw2 = Conv2d(hidden, ch, 1) # pointwise (back to ch) norm = BatchNorm2d(hidden) forward: x + pw2(gelu(norm(pw1(dw(x))))) # residual
This adds ~66K trainable params (for 256-ch SAM3 embeddings).

task.title: RED: Test Spatial Adapter exists and runs

tasks.task: Test SpatialConvAdapter: - Accepts (B, 256, H/16, W/16) input - Returns same shape output - ~66K trainable params - Has residual connection

In sam3_topolora.py (or a new spatial_adapter.py): class SpatialConvAdapter(nn.Module): dw_conv: Conv2d(ch, ch, 3, padding=1, groups=ch) pw1: Conv2d(ch, hidden_dim, 1) norm: BatchNorm2d(hidden_dim) pw2: Conv2d(hidden_dim, ch, 1) forward: x + pw2(gelu(norm(pw1(dw_conv(x)))))

In the forward pass, between encoder output and decoder: encoder_features = self.backbone(slice_2d) if self.spatial_adapter: encoder_features = self.spatial_adapter(encoder_features) logits = self.decoder(encoder_features)

task.title: Test: end-to-end forward with Spatial Adapter

tasks.task: Full forward pass with random input. Verify output shape correct and spatial adapter contributes to gradients.

phase.gate: Spatial Adapter matches paper. Forward pass works. ~66K params.

phase.name: Loss Function Flexibility for Factorial (1.5 hours)
phase.description:
The user wants ALL 3 factorial losses (cbdice_cldice, dice_ce, dice_ce_cldice) usable with TopoLoRA — plus any future MONAI loss. The loss registry already exists. Verify TopoLoRA uses it correctly.
The paper's default loss (BCE+Dice+0.5*clDice) maps to our existing dice_ce_cldice compound loss. Verify this equivalence.
Also check: does SegLab use any exotic loss we don't have? Answer from research: No — they use BCE+Dice+clDice which maps to our existing losses. The boundary loss (weight=0) is disabled by default.

tasks.task: Check sam3_topolora.py uses the standard loss_name config parameter. The adapter should NOT hardcode any loss — it receives loss from config.
Compare our VesselCompoundLoss (dice_ce + clDice) with the paper's BCE + Dice + 0.5*clDice. Document any weight differences.

task.title: Add paper's default loss as a named config

tasks.task: If our dice_ce_cldice doesn't exactly match BCE+Dice+0.5*clDice, add a topolora_default loss to the registry with correct weights.

phase.gate: All factorial losses work with TopoLoRA. Paper default documented.

phase.name: Verify clDice Implementation (1 hour)
phase.description: The paper uses differentiable soft skeletonization via iterated min/max pooling (10 iterations). Our clDice uses MONAI's SoftclDiceLoss. Verify these are equivalent or document differences.

task.title: Compare MONAI SoftclDiceLoss with SegLab clDice

tasks.task: Read MONAI source for SoftclDiceLoss. Read SegLab topo_losses.py for soft_skel + soft_cldice. Document: same algorithm? Same number of iterations?
Verify MONAI SoftclDiceLoss produces gradients that flow back to the encoder. If not, the topology loss has no effect on LoRA params.

phase.gate: clDice verified as differentiable. Equivalence documented.

phase.name: Integration Tests + Staging Gate (1 hour)

tasks.task: All tests must pass. Fix any regressions from Parts A+B.
Full prod suite including model_loading tests. SAM3 TopoLoRA construction test should verify Spatial Adapter.
Single PR with all Part A + Part B changes.

phase.gate: All tests pass. PR ready for merge.

phase.name: GCP Pre-Flight Checklist
phase.description: Verify everything needed for the GCP run is ready. This is a checklist, not implementation.

tasks.task: Check if Dockerfile.base needs changes for: - INSTALL_MAMBA=1 (mamba-ssm for MambaVesselNet) - Spatial Adapter (no new deps, just code)
train_factorial.yaml: - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI from env - file_mounts for checkpoints - No hardcoded region - docker image_id correct

task.title: Create cold-start prompt for GCP session

tasks.task: Everything the next session needs to launch the GCP run.

phase.gate: Ready to run sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml

issue-status.issue: Root cause: train_factorial.yaml hardcodes /app/mlruns instead of Cloud Run URI. Fix: H1 (URI switch) + H6 (GCS mount) + atomic + SHA256 + spot resume.
WRONG: LoRAConv2d — paper does NOT LoRA Conv2d layers. RIGHT: Missing Spatial Adapter (Conv_DW 3x3 + Conv 1x1). Relabel to: "Implement Spatial Adapter + verify TopoLoRA paper fidelity"

plan.execution-order:
Part A: A1(no-op) → A2 → A3 → A4 → A5(verify existing) (checkpoint, ~4 hours) Part B: B1 → B2 → B2.5(LoRA filter) → B3 → B4 (TopoLoRA, ~5 hours) Part C: C1 → C2 (verification, ~1 hour) Total: ~8-10 hours
Parts A and B can run in PARALLEL (different files, no conflicts).

reviewer-corrections.correction: train_factorial.yaml ALREADY uses ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (line 54). Phase A1 is a no-op. Just verify it's correct, don't change. NOTE: train_production.yaml still hardcodes /app/mlruns — fix that too.
file_mounts for GCS checkpoints NOT yet in train_factorial.yaml. Port from smoke_test_gcp.yaml. Plan is correct.
check_resume_state_task() ALREADY EXISTS at train_flow.py:262-302. Resume wiring ALREADY EXISTS at train_flow.py:607-622. Plan should say "verify E2E" not "implement from scratch."

Spatial Adapter goes between extract_fpn_features() and decoder, NOT between raw encoder output and decoder. Operates on 256-ch FPN output. Plan pseudocode is wrong. CORRECT: fpn_features = backbone.extract_fpn_features(x) fpn_features = spatial_adapter(fpn_features) # INSERT HERE logits = decoder(fpn_features)

CRITICAL: Current _apply_lora_to_encoder() targets ALL nn.Linear layers (attention Q/K/V + FFN). Paper targets FFN ONLY. Must add target_keywords filter: ["mlp", "lin1", "lin2", "fc1", "fc2"] to match SegLab's config. ADD NEW TASK B2.5 for LoRA target filtering.

train_factorial.yaml MISSING DVC initialization in setup block: dvc init --no-scm -f dvc remote add gcs gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data inline dvc.yaml + dvc.lock generation Without this, dvc pull -r gcs fails on fresh GCP VM. Port DVC setup from smoke_test_gcp.yaml. ADD TO Phase C2 as a MANDATORY task, not just a checklist item.
SAM3 HF repo name in pre-cache (facebook/sam3-hiera-large) may not match repo used by Sam3Model.from_pretrained(). Verify before GCP.

train_factorial.yaml sets PREFECT_DISABLED=1 and MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1. Rule #19 STOP Protocol says these are "pytest ONLY." Acknowledged as intentional for SkyPilot VM execution (no Prefect server on VM).
